metrics.description: Puzzle abandonment rate — guards against the hint causing confusion/demoralisation. Lower is better; a regression flips the flag off.
Puzzle completion rate — primary success signal from config/flags.json hypothesis. Higher is better; a drop flips the flag off.
Time to solve in ms — softer signal. A sustained regression pauses rollout for human review rather than auto-flipping.
Hint click rate on the treatment path. Confirms discoverability and usage; observe-only, does not gate rollout.

wiring.control_path: showHintButton === false → Hint button is not rendered; hint() function is unreachable. Existing behavior preserved exactly.
wiring.treatment_path: showHintButton === true → Hint button renders; clicking it tracks METRIC_EVENTS.hintButtonUsed then calls firstStepToward() BFS and surfaces a suggestion via feedback state.

notes: Flag 'hint-button' already existed in LaunchDarkly (idempotent reuse). FLAG_KEYS.hintButton and Flags['hint-button'] were pre-wired in packages/ld/src/flags.ts. hint-button-error/business/latency reuse existing track() calls already on both paths; hint-button-used is newly instrumented in hint() (treatment path only, wrapped in try/catch per M08). Randomization unit defaulted to 'user' from manifest. Pre-existing keys made_par and puzzle_completed retained in metricKeys per M05.
